methodology_long: Rankings are generated by a shadow roto model. Each week, all twelve teams are scored across the fourteen active categories (R, HR, RBI, SB, AVG, OBP, SLG, W, K, ERA, WHIP, K/BB, QS, SV+H) based on an estimate of each team's true strength — not by their actual head-to-head matchup result, which depends on schedule luck. Each team receives 12 points for first in a category down to 1 point for last; the Power Score is the sum (max 168, min 14). True strength is estimated with a Bayesian prior (the preseason projection) blended with each completed week's real performance using exponential decay, so recent weeks count more than older ones. Rate categories (ERA, AVG, etc.) are computed from weighted components (e.g., total earned runs over total innings pitched), not from averaging the week-level rates. The Heat Score is the difference between a team's most recent week roto score and its overall power score — positive Heat means the team just had a better week than its season baseline; negative means worse. Final rank order is 85% power, 15% heat.

rankings.blurb: First time at #1. CLAUDE put up a 142-point shadow roto Week 5 — the best single-week performance any team has posted this year — with a stat line that touched every category: 8 W, 93 K, 2.05 ERA, 0.91 WHIP, 12 SV+H on the pitching side, plus 44 R, 15 HR, 43 RBI, 11 SB, .246 / .346 / .440 on offense. The team now leads the league in K and K/BB, sits 2nd in ERA, WHIP, and SB, and is 3rd in R and HR. The structural concerns from earlier weeks (low W, high ERA, weak SV+H) all flipped at once. Whether this is sustainable depends on whether Cole was actually streaming SP starts or whether the rotation is healthy — but until the Week 6 numbers say otherwise, this is the league's best team.

Highest raw power score in the league (123), but the -28 heat drops Burns to #2. Week 5 was a real off-week: 5.26 ERA, 1.31 WHIP, only 4 QS in 78.2 IP, and the bullpen leaked (5 SV+H). The bats were fine (39 R, 11 HR, .286 AVG) but the pitching that has carried this team finally cracked. Burns also took the only loss to a cold Spoons team (8-5-1). Still ranked #1 in AVG, OBP, QS, and W cumulatively — the season-long profile is dominant. One bad week doesn't change the floor, but it cost the top spot.

Holds at #3. The offense remained dominant in Week 5 (44 R, 14 HR, 33 RBI, .369 OBP) and the bullpen produced 11 SV+H. The chronic problem reasserted itself: only 36.1 IP from the pitching staff, 35 K, 2 W. UBC is now solo first in R, HR, RBI, and SV+H — four category leads — but dead last in K and W cumulatively, and 8th in QS. The Crochet-for-deGrom/Rasmussen/Ragans trade is exactly the kind of move that would address the structural hole. Without a roster change, the volume cats will keep capping the team.

Drops two spots after a softer pitching week (4.44 ERA, 1.37 WHIP, only 1 W in 75 IP) despite a strong offensive output (48 R, 15 HR, 36 RBI, .284 AVG). Still 4-1 in actual matchups (beat Duran 11-3) and still ranks #2 in HR and #3 in ERA across the season. The cumulative profile is the most balanced in the league — no category worse than 9th — but no single category leadership either. A solid #4 floor that needs an offensive ceiling boost to climb.

Big rebound from last week's #8 spot. Week 5 offense finally arrived: 46 R, 11 HR, 37 RBI, .263 AVG, .485 SLG — by far the best hitting week of the season. Pitching kept the standard high (2.73 ERA, 1.00 WHIP, 89 K, 6 W). The model now leads the league in both ERA and WHIP. Beat Burns 8-5-1 in a head-to-head pitching duel, which broke their tie streak. Cumulative offense is still ranked 9-10 across AVG/HR/RBI, so this needs to be a trend, not a one-off, to hold the rank.

Up one. Week 5 offense was excellent (.294/.358/.486, 11 HR, 43 R) and even the pitching showed life (3.34 ERA, 65 K, 4 W, 4 QS). Beat Camp 9-5 in the actual matchup. Now leads the league in SLG and ranks 2nd in R and AVG. The pitching weakness still bites — cumulative ranks of 11 in ERA, 11 in QS, 10 in K — but it's narrower than it was. A few more 3.34-ERA weeks and this team becomes legitimately top-five.

Tied with Burns for the worst heat (-28). Week 5 lost to ShawSwank 4-10 in the actual matchup despite holding decent peripherals: 74 K and 4.11 ERA in 70 IP. The offense quietly disappeared (32 R, 10 HR, .261 AVG) and the bullpen contributed only 4 SV+H. Cumulative power score (98) still says this is a top-5 team, but the actual matchup record (1-3-1) and the consecutive cold weeks suggest the model may be lagging the reality. Watch the next week closely.

rankings.team_name: Camp of the New Orleans Saints
rankings.blurb: Drops two after losing to Y&Y 5-9 in Week 5. The Week 4 hot streak (+28 heat) didn't carry: .217 AVG, 8 HR, 4 W, 5 SV+H this week. Still 2nd in RBI cumulatively and decent in K/BB and R, but the chronic weaknesses (last in SV+H, 11th in W and WHIP) keep capping the ceiling. The model has been overrating Camp on the back of a couple of explosive weeks; the underlying matchup record (2-3) is the more honest read.

Held at #9 despite a strong Week 5 (10-4 win over PO' BOYD, 5 W, 59 K, 5 QS, 10 SV+H, .283 AVG). Heat +20 reflects the bounce-back. The cumulative offensive profile is still last or near-last in AVG, SLG, and HR — those drags are why the model can't lift the team into the top 8 even with elite pitching ranks (2nd in K, QS, W). The matchup record (3-2) is better than the power score suggests.

Hottest team in the league by heat score (+28). Week 5 was a strong offensive bounce (38 R, 11 HR, 39 RBI, .275 AVG) plus 7 W and 67 K. Lost the actual matchup to CLAUDE 2-12, but that's because CLAUDE had the best week any team has had all year, not because Balls played poorly. Still bottom-3 in power because the season profile is too thin: ranked 11 in HR, 12 in SV+H, 10 in OBP. One good week is encouraging but the trajectory needs another two before the model takes it seriously.

Drops one after losing to UBC 1-12-1, the most lopsided matchup of the week. Week 5 offense was the worst the league saw (25 R, .208 AVG, .263 OBP, 12 HR was the only highlight). Still 0-5 in actual matchups. The pitching profile (4.36 ERA, 1.19 WHIP) wasn't bad, but you can't win a category league when you're hitting last in the league. Without a roster shake-up, this is going to stay near the cellar.

Down to last. Week 5 was a disaster on the mound: 6.08 ERA, 1.46 WHIP, 4 W in 63.2 IP, 2.50 K/BB. The offensive profile didn't carry (.260, 7 HR, 25 RBI). Lost to Caminero 3-11. Still leads the league in SB — the only category Duran wins consistently — but the pitching is now dead-last across the board (12 in ERA, 12 in WHIP, 12 in K/BB). The roster is built around two narrow strengths and four catastrophic weaknesses; that's not a winning structure in a 14-cat league.
